project.objective: Identify the parents of Patrick Flynn, born ca. 1845 in Pennsylvania, died 1908 in Schuylkill County, PA

questions.question: Who were the parents of Patrick Flynn (b. ~1845, PA, d. 1908)?
questions.rationale: This is the primary research objective.

questions.question: Where was Patrick Flynn in the 1850 census?
questions.rationale: The 1850 census is the earliest enumeration where Patrick would appear by name (age ~5). Locating him in a household identifies candidate parents.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Searched 1850 census for Schuylkill County on FamilySearch (indexed and browse), Ancestry (indexed), and MyHeritage (indexed). All three returned the same household. No other Patrick Flynn of matching age found in the county.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — Patrick Flynn located in a specific 1850 household, answering the question.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Three major repositories searched (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage). No additional known indexes of the 1850 Schuylkill County census exist.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: FamilySearch provides the original census image; Ancestry index is derivative but confirmed consistent.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: FamilySearch (original) and Ancestry (derivative) both return the same household. Two access paths, one underlying original.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Yes — FamilySearch provides original census image with indeterminate-quality information.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No conflicts on the 1850 census placement question.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low — all three repositories agree, and no competing Patrick Flynn of matching age exists in the county.

questions.question: Who were Patrick Flynn's siblings?
questions.rationale: Identifying siblings corroborates the parent identification and may reveal additional sources naming the parents.

questions.question: Who was Patrick Flynn's wife?
questions.rationale: Marriage records often name parents. The wife's identity is needed to interpret the death certificate informant (son-in-law James Brown).

questions.question: Where was Patrick Flynn in the 1870 census?
questions.rationale: The 1870 census would show Patrick as a young adult (~25). If married, his wife and potentially children would appear, providing additional identity confirmation.

items.rationale: 1850 census fully indexed on FamilySearch. Free access, start here.
Ancestry has independent indexing; cross-check for transcription errors.
Third independent index for coverage confirmation.
Confirm Patrick still in Thomas Flynn household in 1860. Strengthens parent-child identification.
Death certificate may name parents directly.
Thomas Flynn probate/will may name Patrick as son.
Catholic baptismal records may name both parents. Irish immigrant community likely Catholic.
Catholic marriage records for Patrick Flynn. Irish immigrants typically married in Catholic churches.
Civil marriage record, if church record not found or incomplete.
1870 census would show Patrick as young adult. If married, wife and children would appear.
Cross-check Ancestry index if FamilySearch search is inconclusive.

log.notes: Found Patrick Flynn age 5 in household of Thomas Flynn, dwelling 84, Schuylkill Co. Three other Flynn results examined — ages and locations don't match.
Ancestry index confirms same household. Transcription matches FamilySearch except Ancestry reads dwelling as 84, FamilySearch as 84 — consistent.
MyHeritage returned no results for Patrick Flynn in 1850 Schuylkill County. Site may not have this collection indexed.
Patrick Flynn age 15 in household of Thomas Flynn, Schuylkill Co. Consistent with 1850 placement.
Death certificate found. Names Thomas Flynn as father. Informant was son-in-law James Brown.
Marriage record found at St. Patrick's Church, 1870. Patrick Flynn married Mary Sullivan. Witnesses include Thomas Flynn (father?) and John Sullivan.
Ad-hoc search for Thomas Flynn death record to narrow probate search window. Found multiple Thomas Flynn entries; none confirmed as our subject yet. Need to cross-reference ages.

person_evidence.rationale: Name matches (Patrick Flynn), age consistent with ~1845 birth, located in Schuylkill County. Only Patrick Flynn of this age in the county in 1850.
Same record as pe_001, same person. Household position and shared surname with head Thomas Flynn suggest parent-child relationship.
Thomas Flynn, head of household where Patrick was found. Age (~32 in 1850, born ~1818) consistent with being Patrick's father.
Thomas Flynn is the other party in the implied parent-child relationship.
Patrick Flynn age 15 in Thomas Flynn household, 1860 census. Same county, age consistent. 1860 census explicitly states relationship as 'son'.
Death certificate for Patrick Flynn, d. 1908, Schuylkill County. Names Thomas Flynn as father.
Marriage record for Patrick Flynn at St. Patrick's Church, 1870. Consistent with known identity — Schuylkill County, Irish Catholic.
Mary Sullivan is the bride in the marriage record. Creates person I4.
Thomas Flynn listed as 'father of the groom' on marriage record. Name matches, consistent with all prior evidence.

conflicts.description: Patrick Flynn's birthplace: Ireland (1850 and 1860 censuses) vs. Pennsylvania (1908 death certificate)
conflicts.independence_analysis: The two census records (1850, 1860) are independent original sources with different enumerators. The death certificate is a third independent original source. However, the census informants are likely the same household member (Thomas Flynn or wife), so the two census assertions may share a single informant.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: The census records are contemporary recordings made near the time of Patrick's birth, while the death certificate was created 63 years later. The census informant was likely a household member with firsthand knowledge, while the death certificate informant (son-in-law) is clearly secondary for birth facts.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: Ireland is accepted as the birthplace. The 1908 death certificate birthplace of 'Pennsylvania' is rejected as a likely error by the son-in-law informant, who may have confused Patrick's place of residence with his place of birth.

conflicts.description: Is Bridget (I3) the mother of Patrick, or was Thomas Flynn's wife a different woman by 1845?
conflicts.identity_question: Is the woman enumerated as Thomas Flynn's wife in 1850 (Bridget, age ~30, Ireland) Patrick's biological mother?

hypotheses.claim: Patrick Flynn's father was Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
hypotheses.notes: Four independent pieces of evidence: 1850 census co-enumeration (indirect), 1860 census explicit 'son' relationship (direct), death certificate naming Thomas as father (direct, secondary informant), marriage record listing Thomas as 'father of the groom' (direct, primary). Awaiting probate records for additional confirmation.

hypotheses.claim: Patrick Flynn's mother was Bridget Carey Flynn
hypotheses.notes: Only indirect evidence so far — Bridget enumerated as wife in same 1850 household. No record explicitly names her as Patrick's mother. Need death certificate (if one names mother) or church baptismal record.

timelines.label: Patrick Flynn — assuming Thomas Flynn parentage

Died, death certificate names Thomas Flynn as father

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched 1850 census (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage — log_001, log_002, log_003), 1860 census (FamilySearch — log_004), death certificate (FamilySearch — log_005), and St. Patrick's Church marriage records (FamilySearch — log_006). Probate search in progress. 1870, 1880, and 1900 censuses not yet searched.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Parentage of Patrick Flynn (ca. 1845–1908)

Patrick Flynn is **Probably** the son of Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania.

### Evidence Summary

Four independent lines of evidence support this conclusion:

1. **1850 U.S. Census** (Original Source). Patrick Flynn, age 5, born Ireland, appears in the household of Thomas Flynn, age 32, born Ireland, in Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania (dwelling 84, family 91). The 1850 census does not state relationships, but Patrick's position in the household and shared surname are consistent with a parent-child relationship. This constitutes indirect evidence of parentage.

2. **1860 U.S. Census** (Original Source). Patrick Flynn, age 15, born Ireland, appears as "son" in the household of Thomas Flynn in Schuylkill County (dwelling 112, family 119). The 1860 census explicitly states the relationship, making this direct evidence. The household member informant was a direct witness to the relationship, yielding primary information.

3. **1908 Death Certificate** (Original Source). Patrick Flynn's death certificate (no. 4521, Pennsylvania Department of Health) names "Thomas Flynn" as his father. The informant was James Brown, identified as Patrick's son-in-law — a secondary informant for parentage. Nevertheless, this is direct evidence naming the father.

4. **1870 Marriage Record** (Original Source). Patrick Flynn's marriage record at St. Patrick's Church, Pottsville, lists Thomas Flynn as a witness and identifies him as "father of the groom." The officiant Rev. Michael O'Brien recorded this information as a witness, making it primary information and direct evidence.

### Conflict Resolution

A birthplace conflict exists: the 1850 and 1860 censuses list Patrick's birthplace as Ireland, while the 1908 death certificate states Pennsylvania. The two census records are contemporary recordings with informants likely present in the household, while the death certificate was created 63 years later by a son-in-law with no firsthand knowledge. Per the GPS preponderance hierarchy, Ireland is accepted as the birthplace.

### Assessment

The conclusion is rated **Probable** rather than **Proved** because: (a) research is not yet exhaustive — the 1870, 1880, and 1900 censuses have not been searched, Thomas Flynn's probate records have not been located, and the identity of Patrick's mother remains unconfirmed (conflict c_002 unresolved); (b) the mother identification (hypothesis h_002) needs resolution before the full parentage question can be closed. If additional records confirm the relationship and no contradictory evidence emerges, the conclusion would advance to **Proved**.

### Citations

1. 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 84, family 91, Thomas Flynn household; NARA microfilm publication M432, roll 810; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 1 May 2026.
2. 1860 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 112, family 119, Thomas Flynn household; NARA microfilm publication M653, roll 1141; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 2 May 2026.
3. Pennsylvania Department of Health, death certificate no. 4521 (1908), Patrick Flynn; Pennsylvania State Archives, Harrisburg; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 3 May 2026.
4. St. Patrick's Church (Pottsville, Pennsylvania), marriage register, 1870, entry for Patrick Flynn and Mary Sullivan; Diocese of Allentown Archives; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 3 May 2026.
